title: This SECRET Settings of MACD Make It Impossible to Lose!
author: SoheilPKO
summary_bullets: Follow my instruction step by step on Trading View platform here.
Click on indicators and search MACD, this one, and add two MACD's to your chart.
And now you need to change their settings.
I want to teach you a secret settings and combination of these two MACD's that together they help you find the most accurate buy signals on the chart.
All right, first we go to the settings of this MACD at the bottom.
And here in the inputs tab, for the fast length, we set it at 100.
For the slow length, we set it at 200.
And for the signal length, we set it at 50.
And then we go to the style tab and we remove MACD line and signal line.
All right, we're done with the settings of this MACD.

extracted_rules: We're going to use this MACD histogram to see when a new trend is forming on the chart.
When it is below the center line and red, the market is trending down.
And when it is above the center line and green, the market is trending up.
If the reversal is real and now in the market we have greater downside momentum than upside momentum.
When MACD histogram went below the center line and became red here, we wait for the white line to go above the orange line.
When this happened, it tells us that probably the pullback ended and the price is going to decline more.
So the reversal is confirmed here and in practice, we could enter a short position at this point with the stop- loss above the pullback and profit target at risk-to-reward ratio of two.
But as I said, this is not where we enter a trade.
And for this purpose, we wait for a pullback because it can show if we have greater upside momentum in the market or greater downside momentum.
If the reversal is real and now we have greater upside momentum in the market.
When this happened and the white line is already closed above the orange line, the reversal is confirmed.
So in practice, we could enter a long position at this point with the stop- loss below the pullback and profit target at risk-to-reward ratio of two.
And in the next step, we are trying to find a low-risk trading opportunity in the pullbacks.
And so because this is a reversal trading strategy in compared to a trend following strategy, it has more degree of risk.
So risk management plays an important role in this trading strategy.
Without a proper risk management, your losing positions will be out of control and you will lose your trading account sooner or later.
There is a riskmanagement strategy that is called 1% rule and it is one of the best riskmanagement strategies that you can use.
The link is also placed in the description and I highly recommend you to watch it now because without a proper risk management, you cannot become a consistently profitable trader.
If you have any questions, feel free to ask and leave a comment for me.

strategy_components.description: So now we have one MACD with histogram and the other MACD with MACD line and signal line.
All right, here we have a MACD line, signal line, and MACD histogram.
And it seems like an ordinary MACD indicator, but it is not because this MACD histogram is not based on this MACD line and signal line crossovers.
The MACDI histogram has longer length for the moving averages and these MACD line and signal line are based on shorter length for the moving averages.
The MACD line and signal line, I mean this white line and the orange line will help us with short-term momentum changes in the market.
And this MACD histogram will help us with identifying trends and trend changes that are happening in the market.
We're going to use this MACD histogram to see when a new trend is forming on the chart.
And then by the help of MACD line and signal line that are showing short-term momentum changes in the market, we're going to use them to find a lowrisk trading opportunity to enter a trade.
As I said, we use this MACD histogram for trend identification.
When it is below the center line and red, the market is trending down.
And when it is above the center line and green, the market is trending up.
So the points that MACDI histogram changes position and color, it could be a signal for starting of a new trend.
But we're not going to trade only based on this signal because still too risky and we may encounter many false signals.
So we need more confirmation to say with higher probability that a new trend is forming on the chart.
Here you can see that the MACD histogram went below the center line and became red.
So this is an early signal that probably a downtrend is forming.
If the reversal is real and now in the market we have greater downside momentum than upside momentum.
The MACD line and signal line can help us with identifying the pullbacks and end of the pullbacks because they show shorter momentum changes in the market.
When MACD histogram went below the center line and became red here, we wait for the white line to go above the orange line.
So the reversal is confirmed here and in practice, Entry: a short position at this point with the stop- loss above the pullback and Target: 2R.
